steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence states a general comparison (‘elephants ranked higher than crocodiles’) and then immediately gives exact rankings. The blank must introduce a word that signals the shift from a broad claim to precise supporting detail.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Therefore': signals a logical consequence or conclusion drawn from prior reasoning. No conclusion is being drawn here — the sentence is simply narrowing to specifics. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Specifically': signals that what follows is a precise illustration of what just preceded it. ‘Elephants ranked higher… Specifically, elephants were ranked third and crocodiles fifteenth’ flows naturally. ✓
  • Choice C — 'On the other hand': signals contrast or opposition. The rankings do not contradict the comparison — they confirm it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Later': signals a time shift. No temporal relationship exists between the two clauses. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
